title: Typedef derived class names make tests fail when used
body:
[t_class_typedef.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/9548340/t_class_typedef.zip)

The example uploaded here is basically the `t_class1` test, but modified to use a class name defined in a typedef.
The modification makes Verilator produce the following errors:
```
%Error: t/t_class_typedef.v:14:7: Logical operator IF expects a non-complex data type on the If.
                                : ... In instance t
-node: IF 0x555556ad00c0 <e53> {d14ah}
   14 |       if (c) $stop;
      |       ^~ 
%Error: t/t_class_typedef.v:18:11: Logical operator LOGNOT expects a non-complex data type on the LHS. 
                                 : ... In instance t
-node: LOGNOT 0x555556ace4d0 <e96> {d18al} @dt=0x555556a285b0@(G/w1)
   18 |       if (!c) $stop;
      |           ^
```
